summary: This is an ultra-short post from 2004 where Shapiro shares a link to a product on Amazon that he considers an unnecessary but desirable power tool. The post is essentially a one-liner with an affiliate link, characteristic of early blogging's casual, link-sharing style.
key_points: Highlights the universal consumer impulse to want tools we don't actually need
Early example of affiliate-link blogging style common in the mid-2000s
money_quotes: You don't need it, but you know you want it anyway.
key_insight: The appeal of a good tool has nothing to do with whether you actually need it.
